item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
Matt Dixon
item.description: The Republican-led South Carolina Senate voted against advancing a new congressional map, ending the redistricting effort in the state for now.

media:content.media:description: South Carolina Senate Majority Leader Shane Massey counts votes in the chamber during a break in the Senate's redistricting debate Friday in Columbia.

item.title: South Carolina’s redistricting effort fails in the state Senate with GOP opposition

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
Bridget Bowman
item.description: Tuesday’s primary runoffs in Texas will put President Donald Trump’s endorsement to the test again — the latest step in a mission to punish some incumbent Republicans he has deemed insufficiently loyal

media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton and John Cornyn.

item.title: John Cornyn’s last stand and Democratic fights: What to watch in Texas elections

item.dc:creator: Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Vance said in an interview with NBC News he is glad the pope tackled AI. He also discussed why X remains off his phone after he deleted it for Lent.

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance's new book, "Communion," is set to be released June 16.

item.title: Vance calls Pope Leo’s AI warnings ‘profound’

item.dc:creator: Justin Goldman
Laura Strickler
item.description: President Donald Trump’s social posts left a trail of confusion this weekend as Iran negotiations continue.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump salutes during the National Memorial Day Observance at Arlington National Cemetery.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump salutes during the National Memorial Day Observance at Arlington National Cemetery.

item.title: Deal or no deal? Trump’s social media posts add confusion to Iran conflict

item.description: The U.S. men's national team unveiled its roster as the United States prepares to play co-host for the World Cup. Former USMNT Captain Carlos Bocanegra discusses the team's construction and what this World Cup means for soccer in America.

media:content.media:title: U.S. men's national team unveils World Cup roster
media:content.media:description: The U.S. men's national team unveiled its roster as the United States prepares to play co-host for the World Cup. Former USMNT Captain Carlos Bocanegra discusses the team's construction and what this World Cup means for soccer in America.

item.title: U.S. men's national team unveils World Cup roster

item.dc:creator: Jo Yurcaba
item.description: Conservatives have long argued they want to end transition care for minors, but federal policies have increasingly limited adult access. More restrictions are on the way.

media:content.media:text: Since President Donald Trump re-entered office, his administration has taken several steps to dismantle trans health care for adults.
media:content.media:description: Since President Donald Trump re-entered office, his administration has taken several steps to dismantle trans health care for adults.

media:content.media:text: Kacey Garner, a former Postal Service employee, is looking for a job with insurance that will cover her transition care.
media:content.media:description: Since President Donald Trump re-entered office, his administration has taken several steps to dismantle trans health care for adults.

media:content.media:text: Aleksandra Vaca tried to get hormone therapy from Northwestern Medicine last year but said she was denied because she wasn't 19.
media:content.media:description: Since President Donald Trump re-entered office, his administration has taken several steps to dismantle trans health care for adults.

media:content.media:text: Daniel Hogan, a University of Florida student, plans to get transition care at a clinic since the university health center stopped providing the treatment.
media:content.media:description: Since President Donald Trump re-entered office, his administration has taken several steps to dismantle trans health care for adults.

item.title: It’s not just kids: Trump’s crackdown on trans healthcare extends into adulthood

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

Laura Strickler

item.title: Deal or no deal? Trump’s social media posts add confusion to Iran conflict

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: Steve Kornacki previews the 10 key counties to watch as John Cornyn faces Ken Paxton in the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff election.

media:content.media:description: Sen. John Cornyn in Corpus Christi, Texas, on May 22, 2026.

item.title: 10 Texas counties to watch in the Cornyn-Paxton clash: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Dareh Gregorian
Sarah Dean
item.description: The Trump administration is proposing a rule requiring federal workers to sign nondisclosure agreements, according to a draft notice the Office of Personnel Management posted Tuesday.“OPM believes that a governmentwide NDA form will promote consistency across Government, better protect confidential information, and better inform Federal employees of their rights and obligations regarding confidential information,” says the notice, which was posted to the Federal Register

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump returns to the White House on May 25.

item.title: Trump administration floats requiring all federal workers to sign NDAs

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
item.description: Tafu Letuli said in his first statement since the incident that he was inches away from meeting a horrible end to his life.

media:content.media:text: Police respond to a reported active shooter at the Islamic Center of San Diego.
media:content.media:description: Police respond to reports of an active shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego on May 18.

item.title: Tree trimmer injured in deadly mosque shooting explains why he’s the ‘luckiest guy’

item.dc:creator: Denise Chow
item.description: NASA announced plans for three uncrewed missions to the moon this year that will serve as early steps toward building a permanent base on the lunar surface.

media:content.media:text: Earth dips beyond the lunar horizon in this irst photo from the far side of the Moon captured from Orion on Monday, April 6, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Earth dips beyond the lunar horizon in this first photo from the far side of the moon captured from Orion on April 6.

media:content.media:text: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman speaks on May 19, 2026 in Washington.
media:content.media:description: Earth dips beyond the lunar horizon in this first photo from the far side of the moon captured from Orion on April 6.

item.title: NASA announces 3 uncrewed missions to the moon this year to prepare to build a base

item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa
item.description: Fatalities have been confirmed at the scene of an implosion at the Nippon Dynawave Packaging plant in Longview, Washington, with some critically injured and some unaccounted for as of Tuesday afternoon.

media:content.media:description: The main gate of the Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. after a tank containing hazardous liquid imploded Tuesday in Longview, Wash.

item.title: Deaths reported in chemical implosion at Washington manufacturing plant

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: Pope Leo XIV’s sweeping warning about the dangers of artificial intelligence drew a largely muted response across the American technology world.

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Douala, Cameroon, on April 17.

item.title: Tech titans mostly silent after Pope Leo’s warning about risks of AI

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: Clarence B. Jones, a top civil rights activist and lawyer who helped write part of Dr.

media:content.media:description: Clarence B. Jones testifies in Washington in 1971.

media:content.media:credit: Demetrius Philp for The Washington Post via Getty Images
media:content.media:text: PALO ALTO, CALIF. - July 1: Portrait of Dr. Clarence B. Jones a
media:content.media:description: Clarence B. Jones testifies in Washington in 1971.

media:content.media:text: President Joe Biden presents the Presidential Medal of Freedom to civil rights activist and lawyer Clarence B. Jones
media:content.media:description: Clarence B. Jones testifies in Washington in 1971.
Clarence B. Jones testifies in Washington in 1971.

item.title: Clarence B. Jones, civil rights activist who helped write MLK’s ‘I Have a Dream’ speech, dies at 95

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Doha Madani
item.description: Are the New York Knicks the best team in the NBA?

media:content.media:description: Spike Lee and Timothée Chalamet react after New York Knicks defeat the Cleveland Cavaliers in game four of the eastern conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Rocket Arena.
Spike Lee and Timothée Chalamet react after New York Knicks defeat the Cleveland Cavaliers in game four of the eastern conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Rocket Arena.
Spike Lee and Timothée Chalamet react after New York Knicks defeat the Cleveland Cavaliers in game four of the eastern conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Rocket Arena.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: New Yorkers rejoice as the Knicks return to the NBA Finals

item.dc:creator: Mirna Alsharif
Erin McGarry
item.description: It seems that the South isn’t in the clear after a damp Memorial Day weekend

media:content.media:description: Strong stationary thunderstorms pounded the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex on Memorial Day.
A flooded driveway in Poplarville, Miss., on Monday.
Strong stationary thunderstorms pounded the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex on Memorial Day.

item.title: Millions at risk as heavy rains threaten floods across the South

item.dc:creator: Claudio Lavanga
item.description: It took a little over a year for Pope Leo XIV to write more than 42,000 words of his first encyclical, which deals with the safeguarding of human dignity in the age of artificial intelligence.

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV attends the presentation of his first Encyclical Letter "Magnifica Humanitas" focused on the rise of artificial intelligence, in The Vatican on May 25, 2026.

item.title: Gandalf, Picasso and MLK: Cultural references ground Pope Leo’s AI warning

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: Tennessee Titans quarterback Will Levis spent an “exorbitant amount of money” in 2024 to investigate who was behind the leak of his sex tape, his mother said in a podcast interview about how she used that incident to launch a mental health brand.

media:content.media:description: Will Levis of the Tennessee Titans in Nashville in 2025.

item.title: Titans QB Will Levis spent ‘exorbitant amount of money’ to investigate leak of his sex tape, mom says

item.dc:creator: Yuliya Talmazan
Anastasiia Parafeniuk
item.description: Abandon the Ukrainian capital or risk being hit in a new and intense wave of attacks

media:content.media:description: Firefighters work to extinguish a fire at the site of a heavily damaged building following Russian strikes on Kyiv on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: The aftermath in Kyiv following Russian strikes on Sunday.
media:content.media:description: Firefighters work to extinguish a fire at the site of a heavily damaged building following Russian strikes on Kyiv on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: The destroyed dormitory building of the Starobilsk professional college of the Luhansk Pedagogical University following a drone attack in Starobilsk, Russian-controlled Ukraine, on Friday.
media:content.media:description: Firefighters work to extinguish a fire at the site of a heavily damaged building following Russian strikes on Kyiv on Sunday.
Firefighters work to extinguish a fire at the site of a heavily damaged building following Russian strikes on Kyiv on Sunday.

item.title: Russia pressures U.S., Ukraine’s allies to flee Kyiv, threatening ‘systematic strikes’

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: Former FBI Director James Comey will go on trial in October over a photo he posted of seashells, provided the Trump administration’s case against him survives forthcoming legal challenges

media:content.media:text: Former FBI Director on "Meet the Press" on May 17, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Former FBI Director on "Meet the Press" on May 17, 2026.

media:content.media:text: instagram post from james comey in may 2025
media:content.media:description: The Justice Department has accused James Comey of threatening President Donald Trump in this Instagram post.

item.title: James Comey’s seashells photo trial delayed until October

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: A 17-year-old boy was bitten by a shark on Monday afternoon while boating with his father off the coast of Galveston, Texas.

media:content.media:description: Galveston Marina in southeast Texas in 2025.

item.title: Teen bitten by shark while boating with father off Texas coast

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best post-Memorial Day deals on mattresses, home appliances, beauty, tech and more from brands like Brooklinen, Ninja, Levoit, EltaMD and Apple.

media:content.media:text: Major discounts on tech, mattresses, kitchen gear and more are still available now.

media:content.media:text: Major discounts on tech, mattresses, kitchen gear and more are still available now.
